mnemonics: DML DCL DDL: Data Manipulation (changing), Control (permissions), Definition (designing)
keyPoints: Data Definition Language (DDL) is used to define and modify database structure (e.g., CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
Data Manipulation Language (DML) is used for managing data within objects (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
Data Control Language (DCL) is used to control access to data (e.g., GRANT, REVOKE)
commonMistakes: Confusing DDL (structure changes) with DML (data changes)
Forgetting that SELECT is part of DML, not DDL
Not distinguishing between TCL (Transaction Control Language) and DCL (Data Control Language)
